description: Overhaul Team Analysis Summary and lineup presentation by simplifying display-only analysis output, improving recommended lineup layout, resolving lineup role species names, and reordering optimizer score breakdown fields.

userStories.title: Enforce PvPoke Reference-Only Optimizer Boundary
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PvPoke-inspired concepts implemented only in repository-owned TypeScript so that the app gains better diagnostics without runtime PvPoke vendor dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: PvPoke Team Builder behavior is used only as a conceptual reference.
No PvPoke vendor JavaScript is imported, executed, bundled, runtime-loaded, or required by app code.
Optimizer logic is implemented in pure TypeScript within existing architecture boundaries.
Existing lineup-aware outputs remain available: recommended lineups, synergy, defensive ratio, offensive ratio, role, and per-Pokemon contributions.
Tests pass.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: @gbl-optimizer and @test-driven-development enforce runtime boundary and coverage.

userStories.title: Add Lower-Is-Better Threat Score
userStories.description: As a competitive player, I want a lower-is-better Threat Score so that I can quickly understand how vulnerable a team is to the meta.
acceptanceCriteria: Threat Score is computed where lower values indicate fewer or less severe threats.
Threat Score output includes ranked top meta threats and overall team threats.
Threat Score data is exposed through the optimizer output contract for UI use.
Threat ordering is deterministic for equivalent inputs.
Tests pass.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: @gbl-optimizer implement threat score model; @test-driven-development cover lower-is-better ordering.

userStories.title: Implement Weighted Top-Meta And Full-Meta Threat Pools
userStories.description: As a competitive player, I want threat analysis to weight top-meta Pokemon more heavily while still accounting for broader full-meta coverage gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: The optimizer evaluates a weighted top-meta threat pool.
The optimizer evaluates a broader full-meta threat pool.
Top-meta weighting is data-driven or configurable enough for season updates.
Full-meta threats contribute to diagnostics without overpowering top-meta threats.
Threat outputs include enough structured data for tests and UI display.
Tests pass.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: @gbl-optimizer design weighted pools; @test-driven-development cover top-meta versus full-meta behavior.

userStories.title: Add Soft Matchup Scoring
userStories.description: As a user, I want matchup quality to support intermediate scores so that diagnostics are less brittle than hard win/loss thresholds.
acceptanceCriteria: Matchups can produce soft scores instead of only binary win/loss values.
Close matchups produce intermediate scores.
Clearly favorable, neutral, close, and clearly unfavorable matchups remain distinguishable.
Relevant optimizer diagnostics consume soft matchup scores.
Tests pass.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: @gbl-optimizer port matchup-scoring concepts; @test-driven-development add focused rating fixtures.

userStories.title: Add Optimizer Caching And Precomputation Performance Safeguards
userStories.description: As a user, I want team generation to complete in under one minute so that the app remains usable in standalone Next.js and Vercel-compatible environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Expensive repeated matchup, threat, or scoring computations are cached or precomputed.
Cache keys are deterministic and safe for repeated generation requests.
Cache behavior does not introduce stale incorrect season data.
Representative generation completes in under one minute.
Performance validation exists for the expected generation path.
Tests pass.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: @gbl-optimizer and @test-runner add cache/precompute safeguards and performance validation.
